title: BitSight Portfolio And Entity Management - Listing, Attributes, And Bulk Operations
applies_to: Retrieving the full monitored company portfolio and entity metadata
Enriching BitSight data with company attributes for segmentation in reports
Managing portfolio membership and identifying new or removed companies

confirmed_facts.fact: List all monitored portfolio companies: `GET https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/companies`. Returns a `results` array (paginated via `links.next`). Each company entry includes: `guid`, `name`, `shortname`, `rating`, `rating_date`, `industry_percentile`, `industry` (category string), `primary_domain`, `size` (employee range), `founded`, and `factors` array.

Company detail fields (from `GET .../companies/{guid}`): beyond the portfolio listing, detail includes `display_url`, `slug`, `confidence`, `rating_details` (per-factor breakdown), `country_code`, `type` (Company/Subsidiary/etc.), and `tags` (custom tags assigned in the BitSight portal).

Industry and size segmentation: the `industry` field is a human-readable string (e.g., 'Technology', 'Financial Services', 'Healthcare'). The `size` field is a range string (e.g., '1,000-4,999', '10,000+'). Use these for grouping in Power BI reports — group companies by industry tier and show average rating and rating distribution per tier.

Detect portfolio changes between pipeline runs: on each run, compare the set of GUIDs returned by `GET /ratings/v1/companies` with the previous run's GUID set (stored in SharePoint or a text file). New GUIDs = added companies; GUIDs absent = removed companies. Log additions and removals for audit trail.

Custom tags on companies: if your BitSight portal has custom tags assigned to companies (e.g., 'Critical Vendor', 'Tier 1'), the `tags` field in company details returns these. Use tags to drive tier-based alerting thresholds in automation: Critical Vendor companies trigger alerts at rating < 600, Standard companies at rating < 500.

implementation_notes: Build a `BitSightCompanies` SharePoint lookup list with CompanyGuid, CompanyName, Industry, Size, PrimaryDomain, Tags, AddedDate, and IsActive flag. Use this as a dimension table in the Power BI semantic model, joined to ratings and findings fact tables on CompanyGuid.
Refresh the company dimension table weekly rather than daily — company metadata (name, industry, size) changes infrequently. Daily pipeline runs only need to update the rating values.

Use the Tags field to drive different alert thresholds per company tier. Store the tier logic in a SharePoint configuration list (`CompanyTier`, `RatingAlertThreshold`) rather than hardcoding in the pipeline script.
caveats: Portfolio membership is controlled in the BitSight portal and via the subscription. Companies cannot be added or removed via the public API in most subscription tiers — this must be done through the portal or BitSight support.

The `industry` field values are controlled by BitSight's taxonomy and may not match your internal industry classification. Create a mapping table in SharePoint to normalise to your internal categories.
GUIDs are stable identifiers but company names can change (e.g., post-acquisition). Always join on GUID, not on name.
